scope: Run a narrower enterprise-only benchmark review batch with one review candidate and one shadow challenger

decision_context.prior_state: The previous benchmark-review run showed ai-security-questionnaire-copilot remained promising but did not yet earn maintained benchmark admission, and explicitly recommended a narrower enterprise-only comparison against ai-rfp-response-copilot.
decision_context.decision: Create a narrowed canonical packet with only ai-security-questionnaire-copilot and ai-rfp-response-copilot, then rerun the enterprise benchmark panel with the questionnaire lane in benchmark review and RFP as the only discovered challenger.
decision_context.why_now: The remaining ambiguity is inside the enterprise-response wedge, not the broader mixed panel.

files_expected.purpose: Stores the narrowed two-candidate canonical packet for enterprise-only review.
Stores the enterprise-only hybrid review spec.
Stores the enterprise-only hybrid run output.
Summarizes the narrower enterprise-only comparison and decision.
Captures the request and verification plan for this narrower batch.

planned_steps: Narrow the focused canonical packet to two enterprise-response candidates
Compare the review candidate against the shadow challenger and maintained benchmarks
Save the note and update the transparent tranche logs

expected_checks: The narrowed packet contains only ai-security-questionnaire-copilot and ai-rfp-response-copilot
The review candidate remains in promotion_review_domain_ids
The final note states whether the shadow challenger changes the admission decision

guardrails: Do not auto-admit either enterprise-response candidate into maintained benchmarks
Make any asymmetry from breakout support explicit in the note
